creator: Dom.Mitchell@palmerharvey.co.uk
text:
Unfortunately, the new python port (1.5.2) has a problem for me.  When I
try to do "from Tkinter import *", I get the following:

voodoo:~> python           
Python 1.5.2 (#2, Apr 21 1999, 15:03:46)  [GCC egcs-2.91.66 19990314 (egcs-1.1.2  on freebsd4
Copyright 1991-1995 Stichting Mathematisch Centrum, Amsterdam
>>> from Tkinter import *
Traceback (innermost last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in ?
  File "/usr/local/lib/python1.5/lib-tk/Tkinter.py", line 8, in ?
    import _tkinter # If this fails your Python may not be configured for Tk
ImportError: /usr/local/lib/python1.5/lib-dynload/_tkinter.so: Undefined symbol "_Py_NoneStruct"
>>> 

I'm guessing it's a shared library problem.  The port's version of
python doesn't appear to use shared libs though:

voodoo:~> ldd =python
/usr/local/bin/python:
        libreadline.so.3 => /usr/lib/libreadline.so.3 (0x280b3000)
        libtermcap.so.2 => /usr/lib/libtermcap.so.2 (0x280d5000)
        libm.so.2 => /usr/lib/libm.so.2 (0x280da000)
        libcrypt.so.2 => /usr/lib/libcrypt.so.2 (0x280f4000)
        libc_r.so.3 => /usr/lib/libc_r.so.3 (0x280f7000)

My system is a

FreeBSD voodoo.pandhm.co.uk 4.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 4.0-CURRENT #37: Mon Apr 19 04:33:49 BST 1999     root@voodoo.pandhm.co.uk:/usr/src/sys/compile/VOODOO  i386

which was cvsupped & built on Monday morning...

Sorry, but I don't have a fix yet.
-- 
Dom Mitchell -- Palmer & Harvey McLane -- Unix Systems Administrator

	"Value of 2 may go down as well as up"
		-- FORTRAN programmers manual
